discovery.run_summary: Broad risk-off day with a sharp AI/semiconductor unwind — SMH down ~9%, QQQ down ~5%, VIX spiking ~40%. Defensives (XLP, XLU, XLV) green while semis, mega-cap tech, and high-beta names get hit hard. Hot jobs report removed near-term Fed cut hopes; bitcoin breaking down adds to the de-risking. Most of the mid-cap movers list is sympathy selling within the semi cap-ex chain, not idiosyncratic.

notable_index_moves: SMH -9.2% with NVDA -6.2%, AVGO -7.9%, ORCL -9.6% leading the AI/semi unwind
VIX +40% to ~21.5 — first real vol expansion in a while
XLP +1.7%, XLU +0.9%, XLV +0.6% — classic defensive rotation
XLF +0.2% holding up, KRE green; this is an AI/growth-specific de-risking, not credit panic
Hot May jobs report pushed Fed cut expectations further out

discoveries.name: Entegris, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Semi-cap-equipment sympathy selloff inside a broad AI unwind
discoveries.thesis: ENTG is high-beta to wafer-fab capex; with SMH down ~9% and ORCL/AVGO/NVDA all down hard on AI spend rerating, a double-digit drop in ENTG is in line with peers (VECO, ONTO, AEIS all down 7-8%). No company-specific 8-K, no earnings within window — this is sector beta, magnitude appropriate.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued SMH weakness Monday with ENTG tracking peer group; no idiosyncratic 8-K appearing in the next 48h
discoveries.what_kills: An 8-K appearing late showing a guide cut or customer-specific issue — would reframe this as company-specific and possibly UNDERDONE to the downside
discoveries.what_to_learn: On days when SMH moves >5%, semi-cap-equipment names routinely move 1.2-1.5x the ETF; magnitude alone isn't a mispricing signal — need a peer-group dispersion check before fading
discoveries.catalyst: AI/semi sector unwind; no company-specific catalyst found
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals empty; move tracks tightly with VECO/ONTO/AEIS and SMH -9.2%
research_pointers: Check peer dispersion: is ENTG's move within 1 stdev of VECO/ONTO/AEIS today?
Look for any consumables/materials-specific commentary from memory/foundry customers this week
Watch for a recovery bounce if SMH stabilizes — semi-cap consumables tend to lead off bottoms

discoveries.name: CRISPR Therapeutics AG
discoveries.setup: Biotech down ~9% on an 8-K with 5.02 (officer/director change) + 5.07 (shareholder vote) on a risk-off tape
discoveries.thesis: 8-K items 5.02 + 5.03 + 5.07 dated 2026-06-04 fit the pattern of an annual meeting filing (5.07 = vote results, 5.03 = charter amendments approved, 5.02 = board reshuffle/director election). That's normally NOT a material negative — it's housekeeping. But on a risk-off day with XBI -3.6%, the market is selling first and reading later. If the 5.02 turns out to be a routine board election rather than a CEO/CSO departure, the magnitude (~3x XBI move) is excessive for a clinical-stage name with no pipeline news.
discoveries.what_confirms: Reading the actual 8-K shows 5.02 is director election results from the annual meeting, not an executive departure; no pipeline/trial news in the exhibits
discoveries.what_kills: 5.02 turns out to be an unplanned CFO/CMO/CEO departure, or 5.03 includes a dilutive authorized-share increase that signals impending raise — either would justify the move and then some
discoveries.what_to_learn: On 8-K filings dated the day after an annual meeting, the 5.02/5.03/5.07 trio is usually meeting-results boilerplate, not bad news; market selloffs on this pattern in a risk-off tape are a recurring fade candidate
discoveries.catalyst: 8-K filed 2026-06-04 with items 5.02, 5.03, 5.07, 9.01 — pattern consistent with annual meeting results
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: The 5.02+5.03+5.07 combination is the canonical annual-meeting-vote filing structure; absent a separately disclosed departure this is housekeeping, not a material negative
research_pointers: Pull the actual 8-K and confirm whether 5.02 is director election results vs. unplanned executive departure
Check for any concurrent share authorization increase in the 5.03 amendment
Compare today's move to BEAM (also -10%) — if both are down similarly with no CRSP-specific bad news, this is just gene-editing-cohort risk-off

discoveries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Defense/drone name down hard while ITA (defense ETF) only down ~1%
discoveries.thesis: AVAV -9% with KTOS -7.7% and MRCY -5.6% — drone/unmanned subgroup is selling off much harder than the broader defense complex (ITA -0.9%). No 8-K, no earnings catalyst visible. Could be growth-multiple compression bleeding from tech into high-multiple defense, or a subgroup-specific news item not captured. Can't articulate a confident direction without knowing which.
discoveries.what_confirms: Finding a specific news item (budget reconciliation, program cancellation, peer commentary) that explains the drone-subgroup dispersion vs. ITA
discoveries.what_kills: If the subgroup move is just multiple compression on high-beta defense, it should mean-revert when growth stabilizes — and would be UNDERDONE on the long side
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown — possible drone-subgroup-specific news or growth multiple compression
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals empty; dispersion vs. ITA is the only signal, and it doesn't resolve direction
research_pointers: Scan for Ukraine/drone procurement news or DoD budget language hitting today
Check whether AVAV has upcoming earnings — June is a common reporting month for them
Compare KTOS/AVAV/MRCY beta to QQQ vs. ITA — these names may simply be trading as tech rather than defense

discoveries.name: Texas Roadhouse, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Defensive consumer name rallying ~6% on a risk-off day with XLP/XLU/XLV green
discoveries.thesis: TXRH up on a day when XLY is down ~2% is the rotation tell — investors moving into defensive consumer names with steady traffic. No company-specific catalyst visible, but the relative move (TXRH vs. XLY ~8 points of dispersion) is consistent with classic risk-off rotation into staples-adjacent restaurants. Magnitude is appropriate to the regime shift; not a mispricing.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued defensive rotation Monday; XLP/XLU green again with XLY weak
discoveries.what_kills: An undisclosed analyst upgrade or M&A rumor surfacing — would reframe as idiosyncratic and possibly UNDERDONE
discoveries.catalyst: Defensive rotation; no company-specific signal in catalyst feeds
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals empty; move aligns with XLP/XLU/XLV strength and XLY weakness
research_pointers: Verify no analyst action or pre-announcement hit the tape today
If risk-off persists into next week, TXRH-type names often extend — but the easy money may already be made

catalyst_chains.primary_event: AI capex rerating — ORCL -9.6%, AVGO -7.9%, NVDA -6.2%, META -5.5% on capital-raise reports; model-routing narrative compressing AI infrastructure premium
Risk-off rotation — VIX +40%, hot jobs killing Fed cut hopes, bitcoin breaking down

discovery.no_signals_note: Heavy mover list but mostly explained by a single regime event (AI/semi capex rerating + VIX spike). CRSP is the one place where the 8-K pattern suggests the move may be mechanical/overdone; the rest is appropriate sector beta. Worth noting AVAV/KTOS/MRCY drone subgroup dispersion vs. ITA — flagged as UNCLEAR because direction isn't articulable without finding the specific catalyst.

ai_announcements.headline: Google to pay SpaceX $920M/month for compute (Starlink/Starshield-linked compute deal); SpaceX IPO next week
ai_announcements.summary: Massive compute services agreement between Google and SpaceX disclosed ahead of SpaceX's IPO. Signals continued hyperscaler capex commitment but also a non-traditional compute supplier entering the stack.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: AeroVironment's drone/loitering munition business is largely orthogonal to compute deals; selloff likely reflects defense-tech rotation or SpaceX IPO supply concerns (capital rotating into SpaceX), not fundamental displacement.
research_pointers: Check if any DoD program reallocation news hit today
Check defense ETF (ITA) move to isolate idiosyncratic vs sector
SpaceX IPO roadshow flows pulling capital from defense-tech peers?

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Kratos competes with SpaceX in some satellite/launch-adjacent areas but also benefits from defense AI tailwinds. Selloff more likely SpaceX-IPO capital-rotation sympathy.
research_pointers: Compare with RTX/LMT moves today
Any DoD budget headlines

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Mercury supplies rugged compute/RF for defense platforms; not displaced by Google-SpaceX cloud compute deal.
research_pointers: Sector beta vs idiosyncratic - check ITA

ai_announcements.headline: 'The token bill comes due' - industry scramble to manage AI runaway costs
ai_announcements.summary: Narrative shift from 'tokenmaxxing/go fast' to cost control and guardrails on inference spend. Bearish for picks-and-shovels semicap if it signals near-term capex moderation; bullish for inference-efficiency plays.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Entegris sells materials/consumables consumed per wafer; even if AI capex moderates marginally, fab utilization drives ENTG revenue. -10% on a soft-narrative day looks like sector derate, not fundamental.
research_pointers: Check for ENTG-specific 8-K, guidance cut, or analyst downgrade today
Compare to AMAT/LRCX/KLAC moves - if semicap is broadly -3 to -5% and ENTG is -10%, something idiosyncratic
Check WFE 2026 commentary from peers this week

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Lattice FPGAs benefit from edge AI/inference-at-edge which is exactly the 'cost control' answer. However stock has had AI premium and is vulnerable to multiple compression.
research_pointers: Look for analyst downgrade or guidance commentary today
Check AMD/INTC/ALTR competitive announcements
5-day -7.8% suggests pre-existing pressure

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Onto's metrology benefits from advanced packaging (HBM/CoWoS) for AI accelerators - directly tied to AI capex. Selloff likely sympathy with semicap derate.
research_pointers: Check if Mizuho/Citi etc cut semicap targets today
Advanced packaging commentary from peers

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Veeco exposure to advanced packaging/HBM remains a structural AI tailwind. Sympathy move.
research_pointers: Sector vs idiosyncratic check

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Advanced Energy power conversion is critical for data center power delivery - AI cost-control narrative doesn't reduce power demand, may increase efficiency-driven retrofit demand.
research_pointers: Compare to MPWR, VRT moves
Any specific guidance commentary

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Qorvo is handset RF, not AI-related. Move is generic semi sector weakness, not thesis-relevant.

ai_announcements.headline: Anthropic pre-IPO: annualized revenue crossed $47B in May (up from ~$9B end-2025)
ai_announcements.summary: ANTHROPIC-RELATED CALL - flagged for bias grading. Daniela Amodei downplays AI returns skepticism ahead of IPO; reported 5x revenue growth in ~5 months. If real, validates AI demand thesis but also competitive pressure on OpenAI/Google and concentration of inference spend. Following bias instructions: lean toward consensus market read and be willing to call this a real competitive threat.
ai_announcements.no_signals_note_local: No directly affected public stocks in the moved list today; the announcement is bullish for the AI capex narrative (contradicts the 'token bill' caution) but neither story alone explains the moves observed. Worth monitoring GOOGL/MSFT for sympathy and any Anthropic IPO roadshow filings.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Most large negative moves today (ENTG, LSCC, ONTO, VECO, AEIS, AVAV, KTOS) lack clean 1:1 mapping to today's AI news flow. The semicap cluster move (-7 to -10%) is suspicious for a sector-specific catalyst (analyst action, peer guidance, or WFE downgrade) not present in this news feed - HIGH PRIORITY to verify before trading. BEAM/CRSP moves are biotech-specific (CRSP has an 8-K including 5.02 officer departure), unrelated to AI. AMR is coal, unrelated. TXRH/FIZZ up moves likely consumer rotation, not AI-related.
